page.title: Argumentation and Academic Tone
page.subtitle_html: Argumentation and academic tone - C2 claims, evidence, qualification, and formal argumentative flow.
page.subtitle_text: C2 claims, evidence, qualification, and formal argumentative flow.

blocks.body: {"level":"C2","intro":"Argumentation and academic tone: compact anchors for balanced claims, evidence-based reasoning, and formal conclusions.","rules":[{"label":"Qualified claims","text":"Frame claims with appropriate caution when evidence is partial or contestable.","example":"It can be argued that this policy requires revision."},{"label":"Evidence and reasoning","text":"Connect claims to evidence without overstatement or conversational phrasing.","example":"The evidence suggests that the effect is limited."},{"label":"Academic conclusion","text":"Use therefore, thus, and consequently when the conclusion follows from the argument.","example":"Thus, the claim requires more compelling evidence."}]}

blocks.heading: C2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page anchors the Polyglot English C2 lesson on argumentation and academic tone.
